---
title: "Everyday Discrimination and Cognitive Failures (Singapore Daily Diary Study)"
---

A seven-day daily diary study examining the association between everyday discrimination and cognitive failures among 484 undergraduate students at Singapore universities (Study 1 from Majeed et al., 2023). Participants completed nightly questionnaires reporting daily discrimination experiences, cognitive failures, and daily stressor exposure, alongside person-level demographics and socioeconomic variables. Multilevel modelling revealed that experiencing discrimination was associated with poorer cognitive functioning at both within- and between-person levels, even after controlling for demographic covariates and daily stressor exposure. Ideal for teaching multilevel modelling, within-person centering, and the study of discrimination and cognitive health in a daily diary context.


## Source and Citation

- **Citation**: Majeed NM, Kasturiratna KS, Lua VY, Li MY, Hartanto A. (2023). Discrimination and cognitive failures in Singapore and the US: An investigation of between- and within-persons associations through multilevel modelling. International Journal of Intercultural Relations, 94, 101805. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ijintrel.2023.101805
- **URL**: [https://researchbox.org/450](https://researchbox.org/450)
- **License**: CC BY 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/)

## Dataset Characteristics

- **Observations**: 3266
- **Variables**: 47
- **Missingness present**: FALSE
- **Longitudinal**: TRUE
- **Hierarchical**: TRUE

## Variable Dictionary

| Variable | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| participantID | categorical | Unique participant identifier |
| day | ordinal | Study day number (1 to 7) |
| age | continuous | Participant age in years |
| age.c10 | continuous | Age centered at 10-year intervals |
| female | binary | Gender (1 = female, 0 = male) |
| female.f | categorical | Gender as factor (YES = female, NO = male) |
| nonChinese | binary | Ethnicity (0 = Chinese, 1 = non-Chinese) |
| nonChinese.f | categorical | Ethnicity as factor (YES = non-Chinese, NO = Chinese) |
| sesInc | ordinal | Household income bracket (1-6; higher values reflect higher household income) |
| sesInc.c | continuous | Grand mean centred household income |
| sesLad | ordinal | Subjective socioeconomic status in community (1 = lowest, 10 = highest) |
| sesLad.c | continuous | Grand mean centred community ladder |
| discrimination | continuous | Daily discrimination composite score (mean of 9 items) |
| itemDisc1 | ordinal | [ITEM] Discrimination: You were treated with less courtesy or respect than other people |
| itemDisc2 | ordinal | [ITEM] Discrimination: You received poorer service than other people at restaurants or stores |
| itemDisc3 | ordinal | [ITEM] Discrimination: People acted as if they think you are not smart |
| itemDisc4 | ordinal | [ITEM] Discrimination: People acted as if they are afraid of you |
| itemDisc5 | ordinal | [ITEM] Discrimination: People acted as if they think you are dishonest |
| itemDisc6 | ordinal | [ITEM] Discrimination: People acted as if they are better than you |
| itemDisc7 | ordinal | [ITEM] Discrimination: You were called names or insulted |
| itemDisc8 | ordinal | [ITEM] Discrimination: You were threatened or harassed |
| itemDisc9 | ordinal | [ITEM] Discrimination: You were followed around in stores |
| dayCogFail | continuous | Daily cognitive failures composite score (mean of 13 items) |
| dayCogFail.m | continuous | Person mean of dayCogFail (between-person component) |
| itemDayCogFail01 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Did not notice signposts when driving/walking |
| itemDayCogFail02 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Forgot the purpose of going somewhere |
| itemDayCogFail03 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Failed to notice something pointed out to you |
| itemDayCogFail04 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Accidentally threw away something you meant to keep |
| itemDayCogFail05 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Daydreamed when you should have been concentrating |
| itemDayCogFail06 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Forgot whether you had done something |
| itemDayCogFail07 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Failed to read information properly before proceeding |
| itemDayCogFail08 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Said something and later wondered if it made sense |
| itemDayCogFail09 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Failed to take notice of something on first reading |
| itemDayCogFail10 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Lost track of what you were saying mid-sentence |
| itemDayCogFail11 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Forgot an important appointment |
| itemDayCogFail12 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Had trouble concentrating on a task |
| itemDayCogFail13 | count | [ITEM] Cognitive failure: Forgot where you had put something |
| dayStressorExp | binary | Daily stressor exposure (1 = experienced at least one stressor, 0 = none) |
| dayStressorExp.m | continuous | Person mean of dayStressorExp (between-person component) |
| dayStressorExp.f | categorical | Daily stressor exposure as factor (YES/NO) |
| itemDayStressorArgue | binary | [ITEM] Stressor: Had an argument or disagreement with someone |
| itemDayStressorAvoid | binary | [ITEM] Stressor: Avoided someone to prevent an argument |
| itemDayStressorWork | binary | [ITEM] Stressor: Had a work- or school-related stressor |
| itemDayStressorHome | binary | [ITEM] Stressor: Had a home- or family-related stressor |
| itemDayStressorDiscrimination | binary | [ITEM] Stressor: Experienced discrimination or prejudice |
| itemDayStressorNetwork | binary | [ITEM] Stressor: Had a problem with a friend or member of your social network |
| itemDayStressorOther | binary | [ITEM] Stressor: Experienced another stressor not listed above |

## Suggested Learning Objectives

- Fit a multilevel model with both within- and between-person predictors
- Interpret fixed effects for discrimination on cognitive failures at the within- and between-person levels
- Explain the purpose of group mean centering in daily diary designs
- Control for confounds such as daily stressor exposure in a multilevel framework

## Suggested Classroom Activities

- Compare a naive OLS regression ignoring clustering to a multilevel model and discuss what changes in the discrimination coefficient
- Visualize the within-person relationship between daily discrimination and daily cognitive failures across participants

## Common Student Misconceptions

- Treating repeated daily observations from the same person as independent
- Confusing the within-person effect of discrimination (day-to-day fluctuations) with the between-person effect (average level across the study)

## Load This Dataset in R

```r
library(statsdata)
data <- load_dataset("discrimination-cognitive")
```
